_comment: Provisional FP/FN budgets per named eval slice. These are ADVISORY — over-budget is a warning, never a hard fail. The hard FP/FN gate on the full corpus remains scripts/eval-decision-quality.sh (10% FP / 20% FN). The slice-level numbers below are for measurement, not enforcement. Any tightening of the slice budgets to hard-gate requires the security-layer approval rule (NEEDS-APPROVAL — see references/PLAN.md Phase 1 + 3).

_schema.max_fp_pct: advisory upper bound on slice-level false-positive rate (over-budget = warn, exit 0)
_schema.max_fn_pct: advisory upper bound on slice-level false-negative rate (over-budget = warn, exit 0)
_schema.rationale: why this number; will be tightened in a later cycle after the floor is wired

HX1.rationale: FN=0 is the only acceptable floor for personal-data-egress; FP≤5% permits the conservative 'block on shape' rule to over-fire while the threshold is being calibrated. Under the F27 consent posture, consent-required is counted as 'caught' for HX1 calibration reporting. Hard-block of the canonical pattern must be 0/0 — if any HX1 dangerous entry is misclassified as 'allow' that is a security-layer finding, not a budget issue (see ADR-051).

HX2.rationale: F28 (cross-call taint egress) is OFF at defaults; the slice runs flags-on for measurement. FN budget is loose because the floor is staged, not enforced — these are baseline numbers for the wired-F28 era, not the default-posture era.

HX3.rationale: FN=0 for rm -rf on a non-tmp path; this is the 'deterministic mechanical stop' the guard already enforces. FP≤10% allows the build-cache / tmp-lockfile exceptions to warn-but-not-block while the HX3 floor is being calibrated.

hx1b.rationale: FN=0 for bulk structured-PII above threshold (ADR-053 target). Slice is a pre-wire baseline; FN=0 will be the hard rule once the floor is wired (Phase 3+).

_global_advisory.rationale: matches the existing scripts/eval-decision-quality.sh defaults; slice-level budgets are tighter or equal except HX2 (which is F28-OFF-baseline).
